text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of working on a whole project at once, teams use iterative development, breaking the work into small parts called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts one or two weeks. Every day, the team has a short standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. Before starting a sprint, they plan tasks from a backlog, which is a list of all the work that needs to be done. These tasks are often described as user stories, which explain a feature from the user's perspective. This way, teams can quickly adapt to changes and deliver value regularly.
